ops.title: A dev build ties with its base tag and never evicts the running daemon — stamp next-patch \"-dev\" to E2E new daemon code
ops.body:
cc-present's daemon upgrade (cc-interact `version.Newer`) compares only the leading `vX.Y.Z` triple and ignores suffixes, so a git-describe dev build like `v0.6.0-1-gHASH-dirty` **ties** with the installed `0.6.0` daemon and `EnsureCurrent` keeps the old daemon — a local E2E then silently exercises the old daemon and its old embedded SPA (hit on 2026-07-12 while verifying the v0.6.1 reply fix; the "fixed" binary appeared broken). To E2E new daemon code, build with the *next* patch version stamped: `go build -ldflags "... -X .../internal/version.Version=0.6.2-dev ..."` — strictly newer, evicts in place (attached channel listeners survive; this replacement path is designed and exercised). A version with no X.Y.Z triple at all (e.g. `dev`) ranks newest forever and is never evicted by any release — avoid unless you want a resident dev daemon.

Related: [[cc-present-ios-client]] (other local-dev gotchas: `task`/golangci-lint are dangling mise shims — run Taskfile steps by hand; xcodebuildmcp CLI not installed — use the CI-equivalent `xcodebuild test` command).
